strengths: Freehold Geran title with 14.8 acres provides rare scale for a landed township in the Damansara corridor
0.3 km to Damansara Damai MRT station — strongest TOD fundamentals among current portfolio deals
All DD milestones completed and SPA executed — lowest execution risk in the pipeline
Landed product mix (terraces + semi-D + bungalows) targets the most liquid residential segment in the Klang Valley
weaknesses: Low 1.5x plot ratio limits density uplift potential compared to commercial-zoned sites
R2 residential zoning with 30% Bumi quota restricts open-market pricing on nearly one-third of units
3-year development period creates concentration risk — all revenue recognition skewed toward Year 2 and 3
opportunities: Damansara corridor continues to attract upgrader demand from PJ and Subang — strong end-user absorption expected
Bungalow component (10%) can be positioned as premium lots at RM 2.5M+ given MRT proximity
Potential to apply for plot ratio uplift under DBKL LSAS transit incentive for parcels within 400m of MRT
threats: Competing landed launches in Kepong and Segambut corridors may pressure absorption pace beyond Year 1
Rising construction costs for landed product due to labour shortages post-2024 could erode the RM 180 psf build cost
Interest rate environment: end-financier loan rejection rates elevated for medium-income bracket buyers

recommendation.headline: Best-in-class TOD landed deal — full approval justified; focus now shifts to launch strategy and cost discipline.
recommendation.rationale: Damansara Damai Residences is the strongest deal in the current BRDB pipeline. The combination of freehold tenure, 0.3 km MRT frontage, and a completed SPA positions this as a low-risk, high-quality project. With all DD milestones closed, the focus should shift entirely to launch execution, pricing strategy, and securing construction financing at competitive rates.
keyConditions: Finalise launch pricing for Phase 1 terraces by May 2026, targeting RM 1.1M–RM 1.3M range based on recent MRT-proximate comparable transactions
Lock in main contractor at or below RM 180 psf to protect the development margin
Pursue DBKL LSAS application for plot ratio uplift — even a 0.5x increase could add RM 30M+ to GDV
